diagnosis:
The failure signature is specific: both cited runs failed in the `sm` persona with `error="json parse failed ... finish_reason=length"`, which means the model response was truncated before a complete JSON object was returned. The concern also records the provider as `model=deepseek/deepseek-chat`, so this is not a generic SM prompt-size issue alone; it is a routing/configuration mismatch between the intended JSON-reliable model and the model actually serving SM at runtime.

In `factory/routes.yaml`, the current HEAD already contains a direct-route override for `sm: azure/gpt-5.4` under `routes:` with comments explicitly describing this exact truncation class. The same file also routes `azure_routes.sm: azure/gpt-5.4`. That means the obvious persona-settings fix has already been made in config, yet the observed run after that still says `model=deepseek/deepseek-chat`. The mechanism is therefore downstream of the YAML entry: either runtime routing is not honoring the `sm` route in some code path, or a different executor path bypasses `factory/routes.yaml` and still defaults SM to DeepSeek.

Because the bundle only preloads `factory/routes.yaml` and persona prompts for the `persona_settings` area, there is not enough source evidence here to identify which dispatch/model-router code path is ignoring the configured SM route. The prior failed attempt also targeted `factory/routes.yaml`; repeating another routes edit would replicate the same class of fix after the desired config is already present at HEAD. This concern is diagnosable as a routing-enforcement bug, but the actionable change is no longer in the settings file available in this bundle.

proposal.rationale: No additional settings edit is justified from the provided source: `factory/routes.yaml` already pins `sm` to `azure/gpt-5.4` in both direct and Azure route blocks. The remaining work is to inspect the dispatch/model-router implementation that still produced `deepseek/deepseek-chat` for SM despite that config, so I am not proposing a speculative second YAML change.

escalation_reason: Current HEAD already contains the persona-settings change that should have fixed this (`factory/routes.yaml` pins `sm` to `azure/gpt-5.4`), but the evidence still shows runtime execution on `deepseek/deepseek-chat`. Prior L4 attempts also targeted `factory/routes.yaml` and were abandoned. Additional source from the actual router/dispatch code path is needed to identify why SM routing is being bypassed or stale config is being read; without that, any new patch here would be speculative.
